category_title: Visually impaired student with no speech
scenario_title: Story-making choices with visual impairment and limited communication
minimal_context: The student is using an iPad app and a communication book to make choices for a story in the classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's make a story using the iPad app. Look at the photos on the screen to choose our main character.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Jane cannot see the screen because of her visual problems.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: I will give you some choices. Is the character a dog, a cat, or a bird?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Jane uses her verbal communication book to indicate 'None of the above'.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, look closely at the iPad screen to see the other picture options.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Jane is not able to use the photos or the iPad in the same way as the others due to her eyesight.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's make a story using the iPad app. Look at the photos on the screen to choose our main character.

STUDENT: (Jane cannot see the screen because of her visual problems.)

TUTOR: I will give you some choices. Is the character a dog, a cat, or a bird?

STUDENT: (Jane uses her verbal communication book to indicate 'None of the above'.)

TUTOR: Okay, look closely at the iPad screen to see the other picture options.

STUDENT: (Jane is not able to use the photos or the iPad in the same way as the others due to her eyesight.)

task.content: Attempting to use the iPad app and making choices for the story using a communication book.
task.learning_goal: To communicate choices of adjectives and adverbs when asked about a character or incident. To make suggestions about the plotline (from given choices).

context.environment: Classroom and sensory room for acted-out sessions
context.materials: iPad, photos, verbal communication book (with 'yes', 'no', and 'None of the above' options), sensory room lighting
context.social_setting: Interacting with staff and other children

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When presented with the iPad screen or photos during story-making
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Jane was not able to use the photos or the iPad in the same way as the others due to her eyesight
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: could not see the screen because of her visual problems

behavior_patterns.condition: if there were not enough choices given
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: She was able to make use of the statement 'None of the above' in her communication book
